security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 170 potential issues including external_commands (96), network (1), and env_access (1). After evaluating the actual content, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick characters detected are markdown code formatting for tool names, not shell execution. The URL is a legitimate source reference. The API key reference is documentation about required configuration. This is a documentation file with example code snippets, not executable malicious code.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Markdown Code Formatting
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected 96 instances of 'external_commands' (Ruby/shell backtick execution) at various lines in SKILL.md. These are FALSE POSITIVES. The backtick characters are markdown code formatting for tool names (e.g., `search_nodes`, `get_node`), not shell execution. This is a documentation file containing example code snippets.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Documentation URL
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected a 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:4. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The URL 'https://github.com/czlonkowski/n8n-skills/...' is a legitimate source reference documenting where the skill originates from.

low_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Configuration Documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected 'env_access' (Generic API/secret keys) at SKILL.md:511. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The line documents that N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY environment variables are needed for certain tools - this is configuration documentation, not exposed secrets.

content.user_title: Build n8n Workflows with MCP Tools
content.value_statement: This skill provides expert guidance for using n8n-mcp MCP server tools to discover nodes, validate configurations, and manage workflows efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Search and discover n8n nodes by keyword using search_nodes tool
Get detailed node information including operations, properties, and examples
Validate node configurations using different validation profiles
Create, update, and manage n8n workflows via API
Search and deploy from a library of 2700+ workflow templates
Access comprehensive documentation for all MCP tools
limitations: Requires n8n instance running with API access for workflow management tools
Some advanced tools require specific n8n versions
Template deployment requires credentials to be set up in target n8n instance
Does not provide visual workflow editing - only API-based management

use_cases.title: Find and configure n8n nodes
use_cases.description: Search for nodes by keyword, get detailed configuration options, and validate settings before building workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Developers new to n8n who need guidance on node selection and configuration

use_cases.title: Build workflows iteratively
use_cases.description: Create workflows step-by-step using n8n_update_partial_workflow with intelligent error handling and validation.
use_cases.target_user: AI developers building workflow automation with Claude or Codex

use_cases.title: Deploy from template library
use_cases.description: Search 2700+ pre-built workflows, get details, and deploy directly to your n8n instance with auto-fix capabilities.
use_cases.target_user: Automation engineers who want quick starts for common workflow patterns

prompt_templates.title: Find a node for specific task
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to [describe your task, e.g., send messages to Slack]. Use the n8n-mcp-tools skill to search for appropriate n8n nodes and show me how to configure the main settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Discovering the right node for a specific automation task

prompt_templates.title: Validate node configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me validate my n8n node configuration. I'm setting up a [node name] node with these settings: [describe config]. Use validate_node with the runtime profile to check for errors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking configuration before deploying a workflow

prompt_templates.title: Create a new workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to create an n8n workflow that [describe workflow purpose]. Use n8n-mcp-tools to create the workflow with appropriate nodes and show me the structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a new workflow from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Find and deploy a template
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the n8n template library for workflows related to [describe use case, e.g., webhook processing]. Show me the top results and how to deploy one to my n8n instance.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick-starting a project from existing templates

output_examples.output:
Found nodes:
- nodes-base.slack (Slack node)
- nodes-base.slackTrigger (Slack Trigger)

Use get_node with nodes-base.slack to see operations like 'send', 'reaction', 'uploadFile', etc.

Validation result: valid=true

The configuration is correct. You can use this node in your workflow.

Workflow created with ID: abc123
Nodes: Webhook → Respond to Webhook

Use n8n_validate_workflow to verify, then activate with activateWorkflow operation.

best_practices: Use get_node with detail: 'standard' (default) for most use cases - it covers 95% of needs while keeping token usage low
Specify validation profile explicitly - 'runtime' profile is recommended for pre-deployment checks
Use smart parameters like 'branch' and 'case' for IF and Switch nodes instead of manual sourceIndex calculations
anti_patterns: Using detail: 'full' by default - this returns 3-8K tokens and should only be used for debugging complex configurations
Forgetting the nodeType prefix - use 'nodes-base.*' for search/validate tools and 'n8n-nodes-base.*' for workflow tools
Trying to build entire workflows in one shot - use iterative updates with validation between each change

faq.question: What is the difference between nodes-base and n8n-nodes-base prefixes?
faq.answer: Use 'nodes-base.slack' for search, get_node, and validate_node tools. Use 'n8n-nodes-base.slack' for workflow creation and update tools. Using the wrong prefix will cause 'Node not found' errors.

faq.question: Do I need an n8n API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. Node discovery, validation, and template search tools work without API access. Only workflow management tools (create, update, deploy) require N8N_API_URL and N8N_API_KEY.

faq.question: How do I choose the right validation profile?
faq.answer: Use 'minimal' for quick checks, 'runtime' for pre-deployment (recommended), 'ai-friendly' for AI-generated configs, and 'strict' for production validation.

faq.question: What is auto-sanitization in n8n workflow updates?
faq.answer: When you update a workflow, n8n automatically fixes certain issues like binary operator structures and missing IF/Switch metadata. It cannot fix broken connections or paradoxical states.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to edit existing workflows?
faq.answer: Yes, use n8n_update_partial_workflow with operations like addNode, addConnection, or removeNode. The skill supports 17 different operation types.

faq.question: How do smart parameters work for node connections?
faq.answer: Instead of manually calculating sourceIndex (0, 1, 2...), use semantic parameters like 'branch: true/false' for IF nodes or 'case: 0, 1, 2...' for Switch nodes.
